---
title: "K08resub_Power"
date: "4/20/2020"
output: pdf_document
---

###############################

# References:
[1] https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/paramtest/vignettes/Simulating-Power.html

[2] https://scientificallysound.org/2017/07/27/cohens-d-how-interpretation/

###############################

# Load Libraries

```{r, warning=F, include = T, results=F, message=F}
library('paramtest')
library('pwr')
library('ggplot2')
library('knitr')
library('nlme')
library('lavaan')
library('dplyr')
```

# Run Power Simulation

I performed power simulations for each aim using the following method:

1) Determine effect size for each comparison (cohen's D) which is essentially the number of SDs the two groups are from each other: (x_bar1 - x_bar2) / mean(sd1,sd2)
2) Run multiple paired t-tests to determine the probability of obtaining a significant test (Power)


```{r, include=T, message=F}

# create function
t_func = function(simNum, N, d) {
  x1 = rnorm(N, 0, 1)
  x2 = rnorm(N, d, 1)

  t = t.test(x1,x2, var.equal = T)

  stat = t$statistic
  p = t$p.value

  return(c(t=stat, p = p, sig = (p < 0.05)))

}

# varying N and Cohen's d
power_ttest_vary2 <- grid_search(t_func, params=list(N=c(3,5,10,15,20,25), d=c(0.75,1, 1.5, 2,3)),
                                 n.iter=500, output='data.frame')

power <- results(power_ttest_vary2) %>%
  group_by(N.test, d.test) %>%
  summarise(power=mean(sig))

# print(power)
```

```{r}

ggplot(power, aes(x=N.test, y=power, group=factor(d.test), colour=factor(d.test))) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  ylim(c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x='Sample Size', y='Power', colour="Cohen's d", title = "Relationship between Cohen's D and Power") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, color = 'blue') +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1,25,2)) +
  theme_minimal()
```


![Aim 1: Reference](/Users/asiri/SynologyDrive/1cbio/grants/ravi.grants/Blaine_CART/image.png)

# Aim 3.1

For this analysis, I will want to determine the likelihood of detecting a statistically different prevalance of CD69+ HSCs in younger and holder human. Look at prevalence from HSC sort in BEM. 

CD69+ HSCs in young donors (BEM experiment): 36/210000 (live lin - cells), 11/271000, 15/327994.
Based on CD69+ HSPCs in MDS/AML, estimate (0.5 difference) 20x more cells in aging humans. 


```{r}
aml.comp <- readRDS('/Users/asiri/Documents/1Research/1Majeti/1projects/1LSC_Genotyping_Phenotyping/scMultiomics/experiments/wta_adt/wta_adt_combined3_H1_H3/HSCMPP_Precog_URD/Integrated_hscp.comp1_precog3_20210201v2.rds')

df <- subset(aml.comp, subset = nmf19v2_ann_gmh == "PRECOG3High")
p <- table(df$orig.ident)

p
mean(p)
sd(p)
quantile(p,c(0.40,0.60))
```

```{r, message=F, results=F, include=F}
# cohen's d is the effect size
# cohen's d = (X1bar - X2bar) / pooled SD
# n is the sample size

# Aim 1:
x1 = mean(c(36/210000,11/271000,15/327994))
x2 = 4*x1
sd1 = sd(c(36/210000,11/271000,15/327994)) # estimate from the above graph
sd2 = sd1 # estimate from the above graph

d = (x1-x2)/(mean(sd1,sd2))

# create function
t_func = function(simNum, N, d) {
  x1 = rnorm(N, 0, 1)
  x2 = rnorm(N, d, 1)

  t = t.test(x1,x2, var.equal = T)

  stat = t$statistic
  p = t$p.value

  return(c(t=stat, p = p, sig = (p < 0.05/5)))

}

power_ttest_fixed <- grid_search(t_func, params=list(N=seq(2,20,1)),
                                n.iter=500, output='data.frame', d=d)


power <- results(power_ttest_fixed) %>%
  group_by(N.test) %>%
  summarise(power=mean(sig))


ggplot(power, aes(x=N.test, y=power)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  ylim(c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x='Sample Size', y='Power', colour="Cohen's d", title = "Aim 1 Power Simulation") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, color = 'blue') +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1,25,2)) +
  theme_minimal()
  
```
```{r}
power$N.test[which(power$power>0.8)]
```

```{r}

pdf('power_aim3.1_diff-in-freq.pdf', height= 4, width = 6)
ggplot(power, aes(x=N.test, y=power)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  ylim(c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x='Sample Size', y='Power', colour="Cohen's d", title = "Aim3.1, 18 needed for Power Simulation") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, color = 'blue') +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1,2000,200)) +
  theme_minimal()
dev.off()
```

# Aim 3.2: 

How man samples do we need to determine a difference in balanced engraftment of 30% (B cell from Irv's paper: https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-07238-x#MOESM3)

```{r, include=T, message=F}
# cohen's d is the effect size
# cohen's d = (X1bar - X2bar) / pooled SD
# n is the sample size

# Aim 2:
# x1 = 1.1 # hr 3
x1 = 0.70 # hr 1.5
x2 = .40 # Empty vehicle/naked mRNA
sd1 = .10 # 
sd2 = .10 # 

d = (x1-x2)/(mean(sd1,sd2))

# create function
t_func = function(simNum, N, d) {
  x1 = rnorm(N, 0, 1)
  x2 = rnorm(N, d, 1)

  t = t.test(x1,x2, var.equal = F)

  stat = t$statistic
  p = t$p.value

  return(c(t=stat, p = p, sig = (p < .05/5)))

}

power_ttest_fixed <- grid_search(t_func, params=list(N=seq(2,20,1)),
                                n.iter=500, output='data.frame', d=d)


power <- results(power_ttest_fixed) %>%
  group_by(N.test) %>%
  summarise(power=mean(sig))

```

```{r}
power$N.test[which(power$power>0.8)]
```

```{r}

# pdf('power_hr1.41.pdf', height= 4, width = 6)
ggplot(power, aes(x=N.test, y=power)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  ylim(c(0, 1)) +
  labs(x='Sample Size', y='Power', colour="Cohen's d", title = "Aim3.2 HR = 1.5, 400 needed Power Simulation") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, color = 'blue') +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=seq(1,25,2)) +
  theme_minimal()
# dev.off()

```

for HR of 3, will need 190 in test
for HR of 1.5, will need 1250 in test


# Conclusions

- For Aim 1, you can use 3 mice in each group
- For Aim 2, you should use 9 mice in each group
